description: A signed aggregate over a set of encounter-family records. Observations decouple ranking from the orchestrator (P6): many observers publish competing aggregates over the same traces, and users choose whom to trust.

main.description: An observer publishes a structured summary of encounters, corrections, and retrospections matching a declared scope, using a declared method.

observer.description: DID of the observer publishing this aggregate.

method.description: Structured method descriptor (see dev.idiolect.observer for the conformance floor). Enables reproducibility and comparison.

codeRef.description: Optional reference to the method's source code or specification.

parameters.description: Method parameters as a free-form JSON object; interpretation is observer-defined.

scope.description: The set of records this observation aggregates over.

lenses.description: Lenses included in scope. Empty array or omitted means 'all'.

communities.description: Communities whose records are in scope.

encounterKinds.description: Which encounter kinds this observation weights and includes. Observer must disclose this or the claim is uninterpretable.

window.description: Time window covered by the aggregation.

output.description: The observation's payload. Shape is method-defined. Typical shapes include correction-rate rankings, quality scores, or structured diagnostic summaries.

version.description: Version of this observation method; different versions may produce non-comparable outputs.

basis.description: Grounding for the observation. Omit when the repo owner is the observer; set explicitly when a third party is attributing the observation (e.g. caching or relaying another observer's aggregate).
